questions.prompt: Which Hotspot tone value is NOT styled?

choices.label: primary
choices.rationale: primary is the default tone and uses the site accent. Definitely styled.

choices.label: warning
choices.rationale: warning is the amber tone. Used for irreversible toggles and destructive defaults.

choices.label: success
choices.rationale: success is the emerald tone. Used for confirmations and post-state verification.

questions.explanation: Only primary, warning, and success render styled markers. Any other value (including info) silently renders unstyled because there is no validation guard.

questions.prompt: Which Astro hydration directives are used by kitchen-sink elements?
questions.explanation: StepThrough, Hotspot, and DecisionTree use client:load. Checkpoint uses client:visible. LessonProgress uses client:idle. client:media is not used by any element here.

questions.prompt: Mermaid sequence-diagram message labels can contain a second colon.
questions.explanation: The first colon separates participant from message; subsequent colons trip the parser. Rephrase the label to prose, e.g. 'INVITE with Session-Expires 1800' rather than 'INVITE Session-Expires: 1800'.

questions.prompt: Click the primary action region in the mockup.
questions.imageAlt: Placeholder UI mockup with primary, warning, and success regions marked left to right across the top.
questions.explanation: The primary action region is the leftmost panel.

questions.prompt: Order the lesson layout from top to bottom.
items: Header (badge, lesson N of M, title, summary)
questions.explanation: The lesson layout flows top to bottom: breadcrumb, header, body, MermaidLoader, LessonProgress, then prev/next nav.

questions.prompt: Fill in the three records that make up the mail-authentication chain.
questions.explanation: SPF says who can send; DKIM signs the message body so a receiver can verify it wasn't altered; DMARC tells receivers what to do when SPF or DKIM fails (reject, quarantine, or none).

questions.prompt: Label each region of the mockup by its Hotspot tone.
questions.imageAlt: Placeholder mockup with three coloured regions across the top: leftmost in blue, middle in amber, rightmost in green.
questions.explanation: Only primary, warning, and success are valid Hotspot tones. The distractors (info, danger) are valid Callout types but unstyled on a Hotspot.
